g.data-narration: Welcome to Servlet and JSP Methods - the backbone of Java web development.
g.text: Jakarta Servlet API & JavaServer Pages - Complete Guide

g.data-narration: Servlets are Java programs that handle HTTP requests and generate dynamic web responses.
g.text: Java classes that extend HttpServlet and handle HTTP requests/responses

g.data-narration: JSP lets you embed Java directly in HTML pages, which get translated to servlets automatically.
g.text: JavaServer Pages embed Java code in HTML - translated to servlet at runtime

g.data-narration: The servlet lifecycle has three phases: init() for setup, service() for handling requests, and destroy() for cleanup.
The init() method runs once when the servlet is first loaded - perfect for setting up database connections.
The service() method receives all requests and automatically routes them to doGet() or doPost() based on HTTP method.
The destroy() method runs when the servlet is removed from memory - use it to close database connections.
GET requests expose data in the URL with size limits, while POST sends data in the request body securely.
g.text: HTTP Handler Methods: GET vs POST

g.data-narration: JSP has its own lifecycle: jspInit() for setup, _jspService() for content generation, and jspDestroy() for cleanup.
g.text: ⚡ JSP translates to servlet with _jspService() method - never override directly!

g.data-narration: Use request.getParameter() to read form data, and getParameterValues() for multiple select options.
Cookies store client-side data: addCookie() sends them in responses, getCookies() retrieves them in requests.
JSP has three main tag types: scriptlets for code, expressions for output, and declarations for methods.
Here's a complete servlet example showing init(), doPost(), cookie creation, and destroy() working together.
g.text: ⚡ Always override doGet()/doPost() instead of service() unless needed

g.data-narration: This JSP example shows lifecycle methods, page directives, and using expressions to display dynamic content.
Remember these key exam points: lifecycle order, GET vs POST differences, and cookie method pairs.
